carolyn.headline: Carolyn Merritt, Yoga Teacher
carolyn.content:
Carolyn Merritt (200-hour CYT) began practicing yoga in 2002 and she has led classes for people of all ages as well as bilingual (SP/EN) and chair yoga. Carolyn holds a Ph.D. in anthropology, has a background in ballet and modern dance, and is an avid tango practitioner. She is the author of [Tango Nuevo)](https://www.amazon.com/Tango-Nuevo-Carolyn-Merritt/dp/0813042194) (University Press of Florida, 2012), and she writes about dance and performance for [thINKingDANCE](https://thinkingdance.net/). 

Hatha Flow is a moderately-paced, breath-centered yoga practice suitable for students of all levels. A typical class consists of postures (incorporating moments of stillness and movement), breath work, visualization, and meditation. Students will build strength and flexibility while moving toward a greater sense of balance, calm, and self-awareness.

Questions? 
Contact Carolyn at merritt.carolyn@gmail.com.

olga-besio.headline: Olga Besio
olga-besio.content:
## Bio

Olga Besio is a dancer, teacher, choreographer and director of tango dance companies.  Her career has spanned 35 years of working professionally in tango, and an entire lifetime of dancing tango.

A short list of the countless ways in which Olga's contributions have been recognized:
* Named an Honorary Member of the National Academy of Tango (Academia Nacional des Tango)
* Received the "Ambassador of Tango" award from UNESCO
* Recognized as an extraordinary dance teacher by the Argentine Council on Dance (Consejo Argentino de la Danza)
* Received an award from the Buenos Aires municipal government

Olga created the first massive and free tango course in the city of Buenos Aires, associated with the General San Martin Cultural Center.  By initiating large numbers of porteños to the dance of their city (tango), she played a huge role in the return of tango social dancing.  She has specialized in, and been the first to create, seminars on musicality, adornments, technique, composition, improvisation, and tango pedagogy.

But her greatest desire is to transmit a profound and true understanding of tango, our dance, straight from the heart.

## Technique & Musicality for Individuals: Multiple Dialogues in Tango Dance
This series explores the translation of sound into tango movement in all its aspects, including adornments.

**Sunday, September 13: The Essence of Tango and its Music inside Our Bodies**
* Dialogues within one's body, and with the floor and the music.  Initiation of movement.
* Weight, axis and the use of the joints
* Seeking a natural manner to put music inside the body
* The birth of musicality
* The translation of sound into movement
* Accents, phrasing, pauses, closures, intensities

**Sunday, September 20: The Music, the Limitations that it Imposes, and Control of One's Body**
* El compás (the tango rhythm or bar in musical notation).  4x4, 3x4, 2x4
* The coordination of sound and movement
* The step in terms of musical timing and physical control
* Technique for ochos, pivots and sacadas
* The motors of movement
* Moving through space and moments of arrival

**Sunday, September 27: Adornments**
* What is an adornment?
* Adornment as part of the essential language of tango, as much for the individual as for the couple
* The musicality of each step
* The step as an artistic act
* The relation with the floor.  Carressing the floor and "sacando viruta" (scraping the finish off the floor).  Strong and soft.  Staccato, legato, hybrid.  Changes of speed in the transfer of weight.
* How the adornment can manifest the essence of tango: for the leader and for the follower.
* Enrichment of the dance, and its limits.

teasers.headline: October 21: Tango in the 1980s - Tracing the Resurgence of Tango
teasers.content:
## Presented by Olga Besio
For people who love tango, it's quite shocking to learn the extent to which the dance form nearly disappeared.  After falling out of favor in the late 1950s, the tango social dance community shrank for decades, until the military dictatorship (1976-1983) nearly quashed it completely.  One of the first things Andres Amarilla told me when we met was that, by the time he started dancing in 1987, there were probably only 100 people actively dancing tango in Buenos Aires.  One of them, and one who may have contributed more than anyone else to tango's return, is Olga Besio.

Teacher, dancer, choreographer and dance company director, [Olga Besio](olga-besio) was part of a small group of young people at the University of Buenos Aires in the early 1980s studying Argentine folkloric dances.  Olga brought to the group the idea of adding tango to their repertoire, and initially, no one else liked the idea.  However, Olga kept advocating for tango, and slowly a few more members of the group took it up.  From that moment until today, Olga has never stopped working on the project to bring tango back. She initiated huge numbers of porteños to tango by organizing a free course at the San Martin Cultural Center, has been named an Honorary Member by the National Academy of Tango, and was distinguished with an "Ambassador of Tango" award by UNESCO.  In short, without Olga's efforts, the global tango community would likely not be what it is today.  All of us who dance tango around the world owe her a great debt.

Please join us on Wednesday, October 21 to hear about the resurgence of tango from Olga's perspective.  Given that tango will need another resurgence after the "tango pause" caused by Covid, this story is particularly timely and important.

The lecture will be presented in Spanish, with English translation by Meredith Klein.

[REGISTER](https://secure.yogareg.com/philadelphiatango/pub?action=shopOfferingsByDate&searchText=olga&offerTypeFilter=Drop%20In%20Class,Workshop)

Oliver-Kolker/classes-and-workshops.headline: Oliver Kolker
Oliver-Kolker/classes-and-workshops.content:
[Oliver Kolker](/Oliver-Kolker/bio) will be back in Philly briefly this week. He'll also teach PATS' regular Thursday schedule with Kristin Balmer.  Here are the details:

amy.headline: Amy Yang
amy.content:
Amy began dancing tango in 2009 through the Temple Tango Club at Temple University in Philadelphia. Having never considered herself a dancer in any sense of the word, Amy was bewildered to learn that she could relate to tango, and thus become a dancer, through structure. Bewilderment quickly became deep admiration and love, and she became a co-organizer for the university club. To further her studies in Argentine tango, Amy began taking classes with Meredith Klein and Andres Amarilla at the Philadelphia Argentine Tango School. Amy became one of the first interns at the Philadelphia Argentine Tango School in 2012, assisting Meredith in classes and helping organize school events. In 2014, Amy started teaching independently, through group classes and private lessons.

Amy’s teaching and dance has been informed by a great multitude of tango instructors. She considers Meredith Klein, Andres Amarilla, Jay Abling, Marcelo Gutierrez, and Carla Marano to be a few of her most inspirational guides on her journey of learning. Amy’s approach to tango is also influenced by her scientific background, and professional training as a relational and systemic therapist. She sees tango as a gateway to learning more about yourself and how you can relate to others.

Amy is well-known in Philadelphia and the surrounding communities as a superb leader and follower, and gifted communicator. Always with a smile, Amy’s structured approach to pedagogy, makes it fun and easy for students to improve at tango and find joy in their dance.

barnes-foundation-tango.headline: Tango at the Barnes Foundation: Friday, January 3
barnes-foundation-tango.content:
We are thrilled to be working with the Barnes Foundation to start 2020 off with a live music tango event. 

The event features live tango music, performed by [Emiliano Messiez](https://www.emilianomessiez.com/) (piano and direction), Javier Sanchez (bandoneon) and [Pablo Lanouguere](https://www.pablolanouguere.com/) (bass), including two full hours of live music for social dancing.  If you've already danced at the Barnes, then you know that their floor is among the most comfortable for dancing in the entire city!  (It's reclaimed wood from the Coney Island boardwalk, and it's heaven to dance tango on.)

Also offered as part of the event are: a beginner tango lesson and a tango performance.  If you are on our mailing list, you'll be getting a discount code in the mail.  If you're not yet on the mailing list, you can join [here](/sign-up).

**Schedule:**
Friday, January 3, 2020
6-6:30 pm: Beginner Tango Lesson (everyone welcome!)
6:30-7:30 pm: Social Dancing with Live Music by the [Messiez](/emiliano) Tango Trio
7:30-7:50 pm: Tango Performance by [Andres Amarilla](/andres) & [Meredith Klein](/meredith), and invited dancers
7:50-8:50 pm: More Social Dancing with Live Music

[Buy tickets!](https://www.barnesfoundation.org/whats-on/first-friday-tango)

Your ticket includes entrance to the collections.

**Location:** Barnes Foundation, 2025 Benjamin Franklin Parkway, Philadelphia, PA 19130

teasers.headline: Impressions (comp. Emiliano Messiez)
teasers.content: Performances at the Barnes Foundation always involve something special.  In 2017, Andres & Meredith performed Debussy's "Clair de Lune," played live by pianist Emiliano Messiez.  In 2018, they performed the premiere of an homage to Debussy, composed by Emiliano Messiez, played by the composer and Nastasja Vojnovic, violin.

teasers.headline: Emiliano Messiez
teasers.content:
Born in Buenos Aires, Argentina, and based in NYC, pianist/composer Emiliano Messiez has become one of the most in-demand tango pianists in the world. He has played some of the most notable music venues in the United States, including Lincoln Center, and performed with some of the world’s top musicians, such as Paquito de Rivera, Dino Saluzzi, and Concha Buika. With Jose Luis Infantino, he recorded the album, “Silencio,” in 2004, which received an UNESCO Prize for music.

With a versatile and unique style, Emiliano performs in a wide range of genres from classical music and jazz, to rock and Latin American rhythms. In the worldwide tango community, Emiliano has become known as one of the most brilliant tango pianists of our time. His playing is absolutely infectious and highly danceable. Whether Emiliano is performing as a soloist, in small group formations (duo / trio / quartet), or directing his entire orchestra, the [Tipica Messiez](https://www.emilianomessiez.com/tipica), you can’t sit down when Emiliano is playing.

Emiliano graduated from the National University of the Arts in Buenos Aires. He later joined the faculty there, teaching in the Department of Composition. He also graduated from Berklee College of Music’s Escuela de Music Contemporánea in Buenos Aires.

Emiliano is the pianist for the hit show, “Forever Tango,” which has been seen by over eight million people worlwide, including on Broadway and the West End in London. He performs “Tango Dueling Pianos,” a unique concept featuring two tango pianists, with Latin-Grammy nominee Pablo Estigarribia.

In 2020, Emiliano released a new CD with the Underground Tango Ensemble, “El Charrúa,” a tribute dedicated to his friend, Latin Grammy winner bandoneonist Raúl Jaurena.

A prolific composer, Emiliano wrote the music for “The Guava Tree” and “Bordello”, an in-development musical based in 1920’s Buenos Aires. He is currently at work on a new musical for Creede Repertory Theater in Colorado.

For more information, please visit [www.emilianomessiez.com](https://www.emilianomessiez.com/).

teasers.headline: Andres Amarilla
teasers.content:
Born and raised in Buenos Aires, Andres Amarilla began dancing tango in 1987 at age 11.  While still a child, he studied with and performed in the dance companies of three of the greatest tangueros of all time: Gustavo Naveira, Juan Carlos Copes, and Rodolfo Dinzel.  After 10 years of intensive immersion in the music, culture and movement of traditional Argentine Tango, Andres became part of a small group of young people seeking to push the limits of the traditional art form. Together, they analyzed and codified the movements, sequences and rules of traditional tango and began to play with the “grammar” of the tango language, thereby developing uncounted new sequences of movements, and giving birth to a new means of teaching, dancing and thinking about tango. This way of analyzing tango has become the basis of most good tango pedagogy in the world today.

A greatly sought-after teacher, Andres has taught in more than 70 cities worldwide, including Istanbul, Beirut, Warsaw, Gdansk, Moscow, Sydney, Brisbane, Belo Horizonte, Buenos Aires, San Francisco, Vancouver, Montreal, and New York, among many others.  In 2008, Andres and his dance partner, Meredith Klein, founded the Philadelphia Argentine Tango School. Until Covid-19, Andres split his year between Philadelphia and traveling to teach in other parts of the world.  Now, he has taken on an important new role as full-time steward of Sheldon the Tango Cat.  Meow!

[read more...](/andres)

teasers.headline: Meredith Klein
teasers.content:
Meredith Klein has been dancing tango for 22 years, including three years spent living in Buenos Aires. For the past 13 years, she has run the Philadelphia Argentine Tango School. Over the past three years, the school has become one of the most prolific presenters of live tango music in the United States.

[read more...](/meredith)

philly-queer-tango.headline: Philly Queer Tango
philly-queer-tango.content:
After a hiatus, Philly Queer Tango is back, guided by its founders Karen Johnson and Elly Fernandez. We are very happy to welcome PQT, which will be hosting once monthly events at the Philadelphia Argentine Tango School!

In the City of Brotherly Love, Philly Queer Tango welcomes all who are interested in exploring open role tango in an LGBTQI friendly environment. Make friends, learn to dance, and participate in creating a diverse, welcoming, positive tango community.
 
Come join us to learn and practice this improvised dance in the role of your choosing.  Light refreshments included.

Saturday, September 14:
2-3 pm: Introductory Lesson
3 pm-?: Stay to dance, ask questions, hang out

Cost: $10

fishtown-salon.headline: Fishtown Salon Concert Series
fishtown-salon.content:
The Fishtown Salon series at the Philadelphia Argentine Tango School presents concerts in a range of genres from classical to world music, and from tango to jazz.  After an amazing first season, we are pleased to present an ambitious and exciting plan for our second season.

### Fishtown Salon | 2019-2020 Season

**STILL TO COME:**

**Concert 3 | Saturday, February 22**
**Vera String Quartet plays Beethoven & Ravel**

8 pm Concert, followed by a milonga (separate admission)

**PROGRAM:**
Beethoven: String Quartet in C Minor Op.18 No.4
Ravel: String Quartet in F Major

**TICKETS**
We are very happy and grateful to announce that we have received sponsorship for the Fishtown Salon series from **Penn Treaty Special Services District**.  As a result, these concerts are now pay-what-you-can for residents of zip codes 19122, 19123, 19125, 19133 and 19134.  Please bring your ID!

For others, we offer these ticket options:
Subscription to the last 2 concerts of the 2019-2020 season: $40
Single Concert: $25

[Buy tickets for the Fishtown Salon](https://secure.yogareg.com/philadelphiatango/pub?action=shopOfferingsByDate&searchText=salon&offerTypeFilter=Drop%20In%20Class,Workshop)

**About the Vera Quartet:**
The Vera Quartet shares the uniquely democratic dynamic of a string quartet with diverse audiences within and beyond the concert hall.

Winners of the 2018 Astral Artists National Auditions, the Vera Quartet is currently the String Quartet in Residence at the Curtis Institute of Music. The quartet received Grand Prizes at the Chamber Music Yellow Springs and Plowman Chamber Music Competitions, top prizes from the M-Prize Chamber Arts Competition, and the St. Lawrence String Quartet Prize from the Wigmore Hall International String Quartet Competition.

Highlights of this season include collaborations with pianist Peter Serkin and violist Hsin-Yun Huang, and residencies at the San Juan Music Academy in Argentina, Verbier Festival Academy in Switzerland, Intimacy of Creativity Festival in Hong Kong, and Rockport Chamber Music Festival.

The Vera Quartet has held residencies at the Aspen Music Festival, Banff Centre for Arts and Creativity, Beethovenhaus in Germany, Norfolk Chamber Music Festival, and McGill International String Quartet Academy. In 2017, they were selected as one of five Young Artists in Residence at NPR’s Performance Today. Recent seasons have included international tours to Argentina, Hong Kong, South Korea, Spain, Switzerland, and Vietnam.

Formed in 2015 at Indiana University, the Vera Quartet was mentored by the Pacifica Quartet as Graduate Quartet in Residence at the Jacobs School of Music. The quartet has also worked with members of the Brentano, Guarneri, and Orion quartets, as well as Pamela Frank, Atar Arad, and Shmuel Ashkenasi.

The ensemble firmly believes in the power of chamber music to transform people, and to ultimately contribute towards a just, egalitarian, and inclusive society.

tango-experience/comments.headline: Comments from Past Participants on Philadelphia Argentine Tango School tours
tango-experience/comments.content:
### Comments from past participants

*"The Philadelphia Argentine Tango School’s 2012 Buenos Aires tour was a great and lasting experience all the way around; expertly organized with just the right balance of scheduled activities and free time. Our Hotel, Mariposita, exclusively occupied by tour participants, was the perfect venue for a first-timer. We had world renowned historical Tango masters from Tango’s golden era come to us for dedicated group lessons! I felt like a celebrity. Plenty of time for breakaway excursions and Milongas every night. The combination of venue and scheduled activities provided a wonderful no pressure, supportive environment yet provided as many options for independence as anyone may choose. I highly recommend this tour for first timers as well as returning Buenos Aires visitors. A great value and memorable experience that will last a lifetime."*

*"I went to Buenos Aires to tango long before I had my dance act together, but I had a magical time anyway. The reasons why are too numerous to mention, but here are a few: we stayed in a boutique tango hotel where even the barista danced, when he wasn’t serving espresso and medialunas; our instructors were patient, demanding, and magically familiar, like glamorous aunts and uncles who just so happened to stop by to dance; the milonga scene was insanely varied and provided ample opportunity to mix and mingle with the locals; our intrepid taxi dancers broke us in by taking us out on the dance floor on our very first night in Buenos Aires—and never failed to show us off to our best advantage thereafter. (Believe me, that’s still a challenge for my partners!) The yoga was great, the food inspiring (even the vegan fare!), and the shoe-shopping scenes flit through my mind even today. Meredith and Monica created a tour with just the right blend of grit and glamour, so that I felt like I was visiting the real Buenos Aires and not just passing through the visitors’ center. Go for the dancing. Go back for the people, the food, the sights."*

*"Thank you Meredith for offering us such an incredible experience and adventure, the group was so much fun and the tango was great :) Abrazos y besos!"*

*"Meredith/Monica - What a lovely trip!  Thanks so much for a memorable week and for your fantastic level of organization, grace and good humor throughout. Looking forward already to 2012."*

*"Thank you so much Meredith, Monica for making this a trip for the ages! Loved every minute of it. Loved the group too."*

*"Am still reliving the wonderful experiences from the trip and telling my friends what a great time it was.  I enjoyed getting to know everyone.  Thank you Meredith and Monica for putting together a unique tour!"*

*"Thanks so much Meredith & Monica, I had such a great time & really enjoyed getting to know everyone.  Can't wait to go again."*

*"Thank you, Meredith! You put together a phenomenal trip and I loved every minute of it! I think you did a fabulous job! I would go with you again in a heartbeat! I can't wait to go back to BA - for a longer period of time. I plan to make it an annual pilgrimmage :-) "*

matias-facio/matias.headline: Matias Facio: Bio
matias-facio/matias.content:
Matias Facio first began dancing tango in Patagonia, Argentina, in 1995 and from that time has completely immersed himself in it. His passion for dancing and studying tango took him to Buenos Aires where he fully developed his own unique style, his dance. His mind and body awareness have been enriched through styles such as Graham, Limon, Cunningham, Flying Low and Feldenkrais. Matias has been teaching tango for more than 15 years, and traveling around the globe teaching and performing has given him a wealth of tango experiences.

In 2008 he moved to Berlin where he now lives. He teaches regularly with Claudia Rogowski, and travels teaching Tango around the world. Matias also collaborates with other dancers like Cynthia Fattori and Mariana Dragone. Check out his [website](https://matiasfacio.com/) for more information about Matias.

Live-Concert-International-Tango-Day-202.headline: Juan Villarreal & Patricio Crom: Live Concert from Buenos Aires
Live-Concert-International-Tango-Day-202.content:
Celebrating International Tango Day, renowned musicians **Juan Villarreal** and **Patricio Crom** will perform LIVE from Buenos Aires in a beautiful tribute to the dance and music we love.  You are welcome to sit and watch, or dance, in the safety and comfort of your home.  

This is a completely LIVE event.  There will be people enjoying the music, and chatting back and forth with Juan and Patricio, in real time from all over the world.  We can't be together in the same room, but we CAN continue to keep the spirit of tango alive and experience the global tango community of which we are part.  In addition to purchasing tickets on our website, you can request a free ticket, or paypal any amount in collaboration. This event is streamed through VIMEO for superior sound & video quality.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/VrruanPWn08" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen></iframe>

## Friday, December 11
### 8 pm New York Time
[Find the time in your time zone](https://www.timeanddate.com/worldclock/)

**BIOS**
Born in Buenos Aires, **Patricio Crom** is an arranger, composer, and guitarist of Argentine music. He studied tango guitar at the Academia Nacional del Tango with maestro Anibal Arias. Patricio has recorded several albums, as well performed throughout South America, Europe, and Asia. In 2020, Patricio will release his fourth album with singer Juan Villarreal titled D arienzo en Guitarras. In addition to his musical activities, he is an accomplished puppeteer, a collector of antique Argentine guitars, and is preparing a documentary film on tango guitar.

Singer, guitarist and composer, **Juan Villarreal** was born in Puerto San Julian, Patagonia Argentina.  He actively participates in different musical projects such as Orquesta El Arranque, Duo Villarreal Crom, Nidia y su Banda, El Muro Tango and Cruces del Plata.  He has recorded several albums and performed concerts throughout America and Europe.  In 2020, he will present a new album with guitarist Patricio Crom in tribute to Juan Darienzo called "D'Arienzo en guitarras".  In 2021, he will present the album "Camaradas"  with Orquesta El Arranque and Maestro Victor Lavallén, historical bandoneonist in Osvaldo Pugliese's orchestra.

Check out Juan & Patricio on [itunes](https://music.apple.com/WebObjects/MZStore.woa/wa/viewCollaboration?cc=us&ids=20455850-1147470507) or [spotify](https://open.spotify.com/artist/255PecelViayzHRWT9aIPD).

lisandro-adrover-bio.headline: Lisandro Adrover
lisandro-adrover-bio.content:
Born in 1945, Lisandro Adrover has enjoyed an international career as a bandoneonist, composer, arranger and conductor.

Maestro Adrover made his professional debut at age 14.  He joined the José Basso orchestra, and toured throughout Japan and the U.S.  He has shared the stage with Roberto Goyeneche, Leopoldo Federico, Susana Rinaldi, and Osvaldo Piro, among countless other tango greats, and performed and arranged for the orchestra of Osvaldo Pugliese.

In 1985, he joined the cast of “Tango Argentino” and traveled worldwide with that show, widely credited for creating an international revival of tango dancing.  He composed and arranged the music for “Forever Tango,” music that has been performed by the Boston Pops, and in the Colón Theater in Buenos Aires. He also composed and arranged for the shows “Tanguera,” “Tango Mirando al Sur,” and “Tango Emoción.”

Adrover has had a successful career composing and arranging for film, including work on the film Evita (1996), The Impostors (1998), and Fermín (2014).  

He has composed major works for and appeared as soloist with various symphony and chamber orchestras. In 2005, he conducted and performed as soloist with the Metropole Orchestra of the NPS Radio Netherland, a performance that was released on CD.

In August 2005, the City of Buenos Aires named him an Illustrious Citizen of the city, for his contributions in the field of culture, both in Argentina and around the world.

His many album releases include: Tango, Esencia y Después (2001), Tanguero (2002), Buenos Aires 2004 (2004), Lisandro Adrover Meets the Metropole (2005), Otra Vuelta (2006), Tango Academy (2014) and Glories of Tango (2014).  Bandoneon Soliloquy (2020) is his first album for solo bandoneon.

teasers.headline: Watch On Demand: A Night With Lisandro Adrover
teasers.content:
## An Online Concert Event

We had a beautiful online concert, interview and Q & A on Saturday, November 7 with one of the great bandoneonists and tango composers of our time, **Lisandro Adrover**.  We are very happy to be able to make this concert available on demand.

intermediate-tango-intensive.headline: Intermediate Tango Intensive
intermediate-tango-intensive.content:
For students who have completed our Beginner and Advanced Beginner levels, we offer an Intermediate Intensive on two weekend days. Our February Intensive will focus on the following topics:

* The *giro* or tango turn
* Fluid and confident execution of the turn for followers
* Traditional accompaniments to the tango turn for leaders
* Alternating between *giro* sequences and movement in the line of dance
* Transitions from parallel to cross system and back
* Simple *paradas* and *barridas*

**Dates/Times**
Saturdays, February 1 and 8
2-4 pm each day

**Cost**
$65

beginner.headline: Guide for Beginners
beginner.content:
### Shoes

Because we pivot a lot in tango, it's important to have shoes that don't stick to the floor.  If you happen to have any dance shoes (ballet, jazz, dance sneakers or ballroom), you can use those.  If not, you are welcome to dance in socks, or buy affordable [dance socks](https://www.amazon.com/Haysandy-Pairs-Dance-Covers-Dancer/dp/B09M9VC88X/) to put over regular street shoes.  In any case, please don't wear street shoes directly from the street to the dance floor.  Please change into the shoes when you arrive.

Later on, you may consider buying a pair of tango shoes at [our yearly festival](http://www.philadelphiatangofestival.com/) or from a source like [https://www.strictly4dancers.com/](https://www.strictly4dancers.com/).  Italian shoes like Tangolera tend to be the best choice now because of their quality and superior padding, but there are many brands to choose from.

### Music

We've put together this [playlist on spotify](https://open.spotify.com/playlist/4k7q4b6bNSQmScSgaz9wn9?si=c89161ec3a4849a8) that is useful for practicing at home.

### Social Dancing

In order to learn Argentine tango, it's really important to practice social dancing in practicas and milongas, in addition to attending group or private lessons.  Practicas are more casual, and tend to be a great way to get your first tango social dance experiences.  Our Wednesday night milonga is also more casual than a big weekend event.  That is also a great choice.

If you're feeling nervous about attending a social dance early in your tango learning, feel free to reach out to [Meredith](/contact) who can arrange for a "buddy" to accompany you during your first tango events.

what-is-tango.headline: What is Argentine Tango?
what-is-tango.content:
Argentine tango is an improvised social dance that emerged in Buenos Aires and Montevideo at the turn of the 20th century. Often hailed as sensual, passionate, dramatic and dangerous, the true beauty of Argentine tango lies in its accessibility. In essence little more than walking in someone else’s arms, the dance offers something for practitioners of all ages and physical abilities. Indeed, tango communities the world over are notable for their intergenerational character. At the same time, the practice of tango affords infinite room for growth, complexity and refinement. Utterly simple and endlessly challenging, Argentine tango is a social activity that can be a lifelong one.

*A Little History* 
Tango is the product of displaced populations who came together in turn-of-the-century Río de la Plata society. Argentina, in particular, experienced unparalleled economic growth in the late 1800s, encouraging a wave of immigration much like that to the U.S. In the port city of Buenos Aires, newly arrived European immigrants, gauchos (cowboys) migrating from the interior, and freed African slaves mixed in the city’s impoverished barrios. It was here, on the fringes of porteño “society” – on the streets, in tenement house patios, in the brothels – that these people who shared neither a language nor a culture created a music and dance that continues to fascinate audiences worldwide.

Tango dance originally developed largely through male-male practice, due to its scandalous profile and the city’s gender imbalance. It remained a practice of the lower classes until it was exported abroad to the dance salons and academies of Paris, London and New York in the early 1900s. Legitimized by the international elite, it returned home and enjoyed a 30-year “Golden Age,” during which dance halls and cabarets spread throughout Buenos Aires, tango orchestras flourished and the most enduring musical recordings were made. A period of decline (mid-1950s-1980s) came to an end with a few key events: Argentina’s return to democracy, post-dictatorship support for cultural programs in Buenos Aires, and the wild success of the international stage show Tango Argentino. Since TA first wowed Parisian audiences in 1983, a global tango renaissance has grown without stop.

This global imprint has been there from the start. Tango music and dance have roots in European, Cuban, African and Argentine traditions, and it was the introduction of a German instrument – the bandoneon – that gave the music its signature melancholy sound. The poetry of tango likewise transcends local culture. Though situated in a decidedly porteño landscape, the lyrics resonate beyond those borders in the entirely human themes of love, loss, nostalgia and displacement.

-*Carolyn Merritt, author of [Tango Nuevo](https://www.amazon.com/Tango-Nuevo-Carolyn-Merritt/dp/0813042194) (highly recommended reading!)*

philly-invades-nyc.headline: NYC Invades Philly
philly-invades-nyc.content:
*Dance like in NYC, but without the drive, when NYC invades Philly over the weekend of January 26 & 27!*

The weekend features Adam Hoopengardner & Ciko Tanik, who are excellent dancers, passionate and approachable teachers, and super-fun people to be around.  They will also be featured maestros during the **9th Philly Tango Fest** and you can read their full bios and view videos on the [festival website](https://www.philadelphiatangofestival.com/adam-hoopengardner-and-ciko-tanik).  We are also pleased to introduce a new NYC-based DJ, Kim Dai, who will be DJing in Philly for the first time.

**Saturday, January 26**
**Location:** Lithuanian Music Hall Association, 2715 E. Allegheny Ave, Philadelphia, PA 19134

**8:30-9:30 pm:** All-levels pre-milonga class – Adam & Ciko
**9:30 pm-2 am:** Milonga Qilombo with DJ Kim Dai (NYC) and a performance by Adam & Ciko

**Sunday, January 27**
**Location:** Philadelphia Argentine Tango School, 2030 Frankford Ave, Philadelphia, PA 19125

**2-3:30 pm:** Delayed Sacadas – He goes… She goes… He goes.  This class will offer something for everyone, including those with 3 months or 10 years of sacada experience.  It will change your usual dynamics and musicality for sacadas, plus it will enhance your technique with turns and positions within the turn, making both your turns and sacadas more efficient! 
*Open workshop with Partner Rotation.  (Level: Advanced Beginner & Up)* 

**4-5:30 pm:** Creating a Deeper, Advanced Dialogue between Leader & Follower.  Focusing on followers’ ability to suggest ideas, to communicate without back-leading.  And on leaders’ ability to listen and respond without losing decisiveness as a leader.  
*Partners-Only Workshop (Level: Intermediate & Up)*

**Pricing**
*Pre-registration is now closed.  You are welcome to pay for all activities at the door.*
One workshop: $35
Two workshops: $55
Milonga Qilombo + class: $20
Milonga Qilombo only: $15

milonga-qilombo-with-live-music-january-.headline: Milonga Qilombo: Saturday, January 25
milonga-qilombo-with-live-music-january-.content:
**Saturday, January 25**

Bandoneonist Raul Jaurena played with Astor Piazzolla.  He has been designated a "Living Treasure" by his native Uruguay. He is also the winner of a Latin Grammy Award.  We are beyond thrilled to have him play in our studio for the first time!

**8:30-9:30 pm:** All Levels Tango Class - Andres & Meredith
**9:30 pm-2 am (or later):** Milonga Qilombo, featuring LIVE MUSIC by Raul Jaurena (bandoneon) and Emiliano Messiez (piano).  Guest DJ Maria Aldrete.

**Pricing**
Pre-registration is now closed, but you can still pay at the door.  The cost is $25 and includes the class and milonga.  Full-time college and grad students get 20% off.

**Location:** Philadelphia Argentine Tango School, 2030 Frankford Ave, Philadelphia, PA 19125

*If you are having trouble finding parking within two blocks of the studio, then drive three blocks north to the intersection where York, Trenton and Frankford come together. There is always ample, free parking next to the Hackett School, on both York and Trenton.  It is pull-in parking also, so you will not have to parallel park.  The three-block walk is well-lit and well-populated.  And we will find someone to walk with you to your car when you are ready to leave, if you don't feel comfortable walking by yourself.*

If you enjoy this event, consider joining us at [Tango La Nacional](https://www.tangolanacional.com/) in New York City on Thursday, January 30 for a super-fun night.  Andres and Meredith will teach and perform.  Emiliano and Raul will play live for dancing.  And we'll be celebrating Emiliano's birthday!

advanced-beginner-intensive.headline: Advanced Beginner Intensive (Level 2)
advanced-beginner-intensive.content:
The Advanced Beginner Intensive is for students who have taken our Beginner Intensive, our weekly beginner classes (approximately 8 weeks), or have equivalent experience

In addition to reviewing and solidifying material from the beginner level, the Intensive covers topics crucial in preparing students for our regular Intermediate-level group classes. Topics include: cross system, the cross basic step, the sandwich (both sides), simple barridas, front and back ochos, and simple walks in parallel and cross system. A primary goal of this intensive is to help students gain comfort with simple improvisation so that they may both take regular Intermediate-level group classes, and enjoy social dancing.  

**Next Advanced Beginner Intensive:**

Saturday, January 11: 2-4 pm - Session 1
Sunday, January 12: 2-4 pm - Session 2

Taught by [Andres Amarilla](/andres) & [Meredith Klein](/meredith)

[REGISTER NOW](https://secure.yogareg.com/philadelphiatango/pub?action=shopOneWorkshop&cartID=&workshopID=2463e15d7d05000047a9e0201fac0000&partID=2463e15d7e05000047a9e0201fac0000&regOptionID=)

privacy-policy.headline: Privacy Policy
privacy-policy.content:
**What information do we collect?**

We collect information from you when you place an order or subscribe to our newsletter. When submitting a [registration](/registration), you may be asked to enter your name, e-mail address, mailing address, phone number or credit card information. You may, however, visit our site anonymously.

**What do we use your information for?**

Any of the information we collect from you may be used in one of the following ways: 
* To improve customer service: Your information helps us to more effectively respond to your customer service requests and support needs.
* To process transactions: Your information, whether public or private, will not be sold, exchanged, transferred, or given to any other company for any reason whatsoever, without your consent, other than for the express purpose of delivering the purchased product or service requested.
* To send periodic emails: The email address you provide for order processing, will only be used to send you information and updates pertaining to your order.  Note: If at any time you would like to unsubscribe from receiving future emails, we include detailed unsubscribe instructions at the bottom of each email.

**How do we protect your information?**

We implement a variety of security measures to maintain the safety of your personal information when you place an order.

We offer the use of a secure server. All supplied sensitive/credit information is transmitted via Secure Socket Layer (SSL) technology and then encrypted into our Payment gateway providers database only to be accessible by those authorized with special access rights to such systems, and are required to keep the information confidential.

**Do we use cookies?** 

Yes.

**Do we disclose any information to outside parties?**

We do not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer to outside parties your personally identifiable information. This does not include trusted third parties who assist us in operating our website, conducting our business, or providing registration/database services, so long as those parties agree to keep this information confidential. We may also release your information when we believe release is appropriate to comply with the law, enforce our site policies, or protect ours or others rights, property, or safety. However, non-personally identifiable visitor information may be provided to other parties for marketing, advertising, or other uses.

**Childrens Online Privacy Protection Act Compliance**

We are in compliance with the requirements of COPPA (Childrens Online Privacy Protection Act), we do not collect any information from anyone under 13 years of age. Our website, products and services are all directed to people who are at least 13 years old or older.

**Online Privacy Policy Only**

This online privacy policy applies only to information collected through our website and not to information collected offline.

**Your Consent** 

By using our site, you consent to our privacy policy.

**Changes to our Privacy Policy**

If we decide to change our privacy policy, we will post those changes on this page.  This policy was last modified on January 22, 2021.

**Contacting Us**

If there are any questions regarding this privacy policy you may contact us using the information below.

Meredith Klein, Director
Philadelphia Argentine Tango School
2030 Frankford Ave
Philadelphia, PA 19125 USA
meredithklein@gmail.com
617-291-3798

gustavo-rember/bio.headline: Gustavo Rember: Bio
gustavo-rember/bio.content:
**BACKGROUND**

My first contact with tango was, as a kid, watching a TV show with dad, mom and my little sister (who turned into pro tango dancer years later). On the screen, Osvaldo Pugliese and his orchestra in all their splendor were playing a tango that, I fantasize, might have been La Yumba.

During many years, I did not lose contact with tango, but I followed my passion for singing rock music. So it was not until 2004 that I started taking tango dance more seriously. I remember attending regularly four to six classes a week as I was determined to get dances someday with the best dancers I could, knowing the challenge to make it way.

**MY MAESTROS**

Gabriela Elías (Mariano Mores choreographer), Horacio Godoy, Cecilia García, Elina Ruiz, Alejando Larenas, Marisol Morales, Natacha Poberaj, Moira Castellano, Mariano Chicho Frúmboli, Juana Sepúlveda, just to name those whom I’ve taken more classes with.

**TEACHING TANGO**

Regular classes at Estudio 4030 (2012), Milonga Pipetuá (2012), Estudio 2 Perfiles (2013), La Mandrilera (2015), Escuela Argentina de Tango (2015/16), and Estudio El Metejón (2016/17). Guest teacher at Tangology (London), Milonga la T (Malmö), with Gaby Farías (Montevideo) and Alberto Germanó (Tucumán, Argentina).

My dear partners in teaching: Cecilia Berra (today H. Godoy’s partner), Lorena Oviedo, Natalia Viola, Cecilia Piccinni, Gaby Mataloni, Paula Pandol, and, specially, Elina Ruiz.
 
**HOW I FEEL ABOUT DANCING TANGO**

I do not feel dancing tango as a job. It’s more like a means for expressing yourself, your mood, your passion, your past, present and future. And, above all, as the possibility of sharing honest moments through music, embraces, laughter, pain and joy, which are the very roots of tango culture.

alexei.headline: Alexei Borovik, Classical Ballet Teacher
alexei.content:
Alexei Borovik was born in Russia and began his ballet training at the age of ten at the Perm Ballet Academy. Upon graduating in 1979, he was invited to join the Perm Ballet Theatre. His reputation quickly established itself, and in 1982 he was elevated to Premiere Dancer. Alexei was awarded the Second Prize at The Artistic Ballet Competition in Moscow in 1984. In 1987, he won the Gold Medal at The Sixth Concourse International Ballet Festival in Peru and was honored with a decoration as "Merited Artist of Russia".

A highly sought-after guest artist, Alexei has made numerous appearances internationally and throughout Russia. In 1997, he toured China with The Stars of the American Ballet. From 1992-2006, Alexei was a Principal Dancer with the Pennsylvania Ballet. His extensive repertoire includes principal roles in Giselle, Swan Lake, Romeo and Juliet, Cinderella, Sleeping Beauty, Coppelia, Don Quixote, and The Nutcracker, among others. Along with leading roles in the classical repertoire, he has taken up additional assignments in the ballets of George Balanchine, including Apollo, Rubies, The Four Temperaments, Tarantella, Serenade, Theme and Variations, and Stars and Stripes. Alexei has also danced in such diverse pieces as Hans van Manen’s Grosse Fugue, Paul Taylor’s Arden Court, Lynne Taylor Corbett’s Great Galloping Gottschalk, and has created roles in many new ballets. In June 1997, Alexei had the prestigious honor of being invited to return to his homeland of Russia to appear as Principal Guest Artist in The Perm Ballet Theatre’s productions of Don Quixote and Giselle in celebration of their 125th Jubilee. 

On October 13, 2006, Alexei retired from the Pennsylvania Ballet with a Gala Performance of Balanchine’s Apollo. He gave his last performance with Perm Ballet in Russia in November 2006. Alexei has worked with Jeanne Ruddy Dance since January 2007, teaching ballet classes and expanding his career as a modern dancer. He is on the faculty as ballet teacher with the Pennsylvania Ballet School. 

Alexei is very pleased to have the opportunity to work with all levels of students and is committed to providing the highest-quality ballet training in a fun and supportive environment. Alexei teaches Saturday morning ballet classes for beginners and all levels at PATS.  A new series starts in the fall of 2018.

[Go to Alexei's website](http://alexeiborovik.com/)

How-to-dance-to-Romantica-Milonguera.headline: How to Dance to Romantica Milonguera
How-to-dance-to-Romantica-Milonguera.content:
Join [Kristin Balmer](/kristin) and [Elly Fernandez](/elly) for four nights of classes to help you get ready for September 21, when the "World's Best Tango Orchestra" (*Premios Tango 2019*) comes to Philadelphia, [Orquesta Romantica Milonguera](romantica-milonguera-in-philadelphia-september-21).

**Monday, August 19**
7-8 pm: Tango Technique: Exploring size, speed, and level in ochos to [Invierno](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U2fv4i1fo8g) & [And I Love Her](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=G4f8mNro28E)
8-9:30 pm: Intermediate Tango: Integrating the 4 dance elements (size, speed, level, and order) in ocho combinations to evoke specific emotions in [Invierno](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U2fv4i1fo8g)

**Wednesday, August 21**
7:30-9 pm: Int/Advanced Tango: Using the 4 elements of dance to improvise with circular movements to [El Reloj](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PPQ-FnAY-ns)

**Monday, August 26**
7-8 pm: Tango Technique: Exploring the 4 dance elements in your walk in [Que te importa que te llore](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XiJAi31hHfk)
8-9:30 pm: Intermediate Tango: Integrating the 4 dance elements in your walk to evoke specific emotions in [Remolino](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Fqm-QEcwXkU)

**Wednesday, August 28**
7:30-9 pm: Creating a super salida by exploring various levels of movement, inspired by [Solamente Ella](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KoHoknZNiaQ)

Oliver-Kolker/bio.headline: Oliver Kolker: Bio
Oliver-Kolker/bio.content:
A native-born New Yorker, Oliver Kolker grew up in Argentina from the age of 3.  He started dancing tango in 1997 and has enjoyed success in many different areas of tango. 

Oliver has taught & performed worldwide, becoming famous in particular for his infectious and fantastic performances of milonga.  He served as Executive Director of Mora Godoy’s tango school and created the “Tango Emocion” company and show with her.  He has placed highly in both salon and stage categories of the World Tango Championship.  In 2014, Oliver achieved a long-worked-for dream when he wrote, directed and acted in the acclaimed movie FERMIN, Glories of Tango , which was presented in more than 10 festivals around the world.  (It is a beautiful movie!)  Oliver continues to split his time between the acting/film world and the tango teaching/dancing world. 

Oliver taught at PATS many times in our early days, but since he moved back to Buenos Aires, we have not seen him in Philly.  We miss him & are very excited to welcome here again!

maja-and-marko.headline: Maja Petrović and Marko Miljević
maja-and-marko.content:
**Maja Petrović** has been dancing since she was three years old. She has a background in contemporary dance and ballet, as well as latin and ballroom dances. She attended music school for ten years, where she played the piano and flute. She studied Spanish and German language and literature at the University of Zagreb and worked as language teacher and translator. In Karlsruhe, she worked in different departments of the Center for Art and Media Technology (ZKM). 

**Marko Miljević** was more into sports and almost went pro in basketball. Instead, he decided to study Electrotechnics and later Physics in which he obtained a degree at the University of Zagreb. He later continued his education and in 2014 received his PhD in Nanobiotechnology at the Karlsruhe Institute of Technology (KIT). From 2010 until 2018 we have lived in Karlsruhe, Germany. We curently live in Zagreb, Croatia.

We started dancing tango in Zagreb, Croatia in 2002 in a small group of enthusiasts curious about this “new” dance. Some five years later we began giving regular lessons at the Tango Argentino Zagreb dance school. Our first and most influential teachers were Pablo Rodríguez and Noelia Hurtado. Later we continued to study with Noelia and Carlitos Espinoza. We also studied with Federico Naveira and Inés Muzzoppapa, Bruno Tombari and Mariangeles Caamaño and Chicho Frumboli and Juana Sepúlveda, among others.

Since 2014 we are working with tango professionally, holding workshops and performing all over Europe (Amsterdam, Minsk, Florence, Karlsruhe, Genova, Budapest, L´Aquila, Freiburg, Treviso, Poreč, Mantova, Halle, Sevilla, Lisbon, Procida, Moscow, St. Petersburg, Reykjavik etc.). In 2018 we have started to work in North America, Asia and Australia.

youth-tango.headline: Youth Tango at PATS
youth-tango.content:
**Youth Tango in 2019**

We are working on starting a Youth Tango program at PATS.  Depending on kids' availability, this may take the form of a weekend retreat (usually a Saturday or Sunday from 2-4 pm), or a weekly class (45 minutes to 1 hour). We are targeting the classes to two different ages groups:

Teens: ages 12-17
Kids: ages 7-11

If your child or teen may be interested in participating, please email meredithklein@gmail.com!

**Benefits of the Youth Tango Program**

Youth tango at PATS is not just about learning steps!  Our interdisciplinary classes integrate this unique partner dance with Improvisation and Leadership Skills development. The benefits of dancing tango are many - youth will cultivate self-confidence, boost their verbal and non-verbal communication skills, engage their sense of belonging, foster awareness of the space around them, and explore various ways to solve problems on and off the dance floor. We strongly believe in playfully exploring where this remarkable dance can take us - come join the fun! 

Taught by [Kristin Balmer](/kristin) & [Elly Fernandez](/elly).

diana-cruz.headline: Diana Cruz
diana-cruz.content:
## Bio

Ms. Cruz is a dancer, performer, teacher, choreographer and —most importantly— an ongoing student of Movement methods and expressions. Originally from Paraguay, currently based in Argentina, she danced for various ballet, contemporary and modern dance companies in Paraguay, Argentina and the United States. She is a certified Comprehensive Pilates Instructor by BASI (Body Arts and Science International). Her discovery of Argentine Tango has given her yet another turn in her endless exploration journey of new ways of moving and expressing. She joined Tango dancer Nick Jones in an artistic partnership that forms NaturalTango, a Tango Dance and Music ensamble —Ms. Cruz is a singer for Orquesta Típica NaturalTango directed by bandoneón player Nick Jones— that toured and continue touring around the United States, Europe, Asia and South América. Diana is a dancer, artistic collaborator and board member with Parasol Arts, a non-profit organization for promoting Tango arts, based in Denver, CO. She has also directed and produced several theatre dance productions under NaturalTango and Cruzartango (her solo project).

Ms. Cruz incorporates her multidisciplinary techniques (Ballet, Contemporary Dance, Modern Dance, Latin American Folk dances, Tango Dance, Pilates, Yoga, Singing, Voice Training) into one cohesive dance. She uses these techniques to create powerful, musical, unique performances. Her continuing learning and understanding of biomechanics gives her an astute approach as a movement teacher. She holds a degree in Psychology, study that has enhanced her comprehension of the human mind and body dynamics.

Ms. Cruz has created her own line of Tango dance shoes for women, under Cruzartango.

virtual-tango-festival-zoom-help.headline: Advice for the Best Virtual Fest Experience
virtual-tango-festival-zoom-help.content:
Thanks for bravely joining us on this crazy experiment to hold a tango festival over Zoom.  This page will provide advice for having the best experience possible.

**For most of you, your zoom links are in the receipt(s) that got emailed to you when you registered.**  If you registered before we had the zoom links figured out, you have received a personal email from me.  If you can't find the email, check your spam and promotions folders.  You can also [login to your yogareg account](https://secure.yogareg.com/philadelphiatango/pub?action=shopOfferingsByDate&offerTypeFilter=Drop%20In%20Class,Workshop) to view your receipt.

**Please never share any zoom links in a public format** like social media or on a website.  There are people looking for those links to cause mischief, and we want to keep that away from the festival.  Thank you!

**Before the festival**

* Download the latest version of Zoom before the festival [https://zoom.us/download](https://zoom.us/download).
* Check that you have the latest version by clicking your profile picture in the top right corner, then click “Check for Updates.”
* It is recommended to use zoom on a computer when possible, rather than on a mobile device.
* You can test your device and configuration settings before the meeting: [https://zoom.us/test](https://zoom.us/test).
* Find a headset with headphones/microphone and use that when you are sitting in front of the computer for a lecture.
* Figure out how to connect your computer to a good sound system for the concerts (or use headphones).  The concerts will sound much better through good speakers or headphones, rather than through your computer speakers.
* Test out the space where you are going to dance (if you are taking dance classes).  Make sure you have enough space and a smooth floor.  Make sure you can hear the computer audio and see the screen.
* All registrants should now have their zoom links.  Please [email Meredith](mailto:meredithklein@gmail.com) immediately if you are missing the links you need to logon to the activities your registered for. 

**During the festival**
* [World Clock](https://www.timeanddate.com/worldclock/) so you know when activities are happening in your local time zone.
* Use the link that has been sent to you by email to login to each activity you are registered for.  (The links for free activities will also be sent to all registrants.)  **Each festival activity has its own link.**
* Enter the meeting ID, password, and your display name when prompted.
		- If you’re already signed in, change your name if you don’t want your default name to appear.
		- If you’re not signed in, simply enter your name as you wish it to appear.
		- Select if you would like to connect audio and/or video and click **Join**.
* Check out [this great PDF](https://drive.google.com/open?id=1UJb13HDmcRhJS5IxurxsX5ZE8TfHr9FZ) with screen shots for each step, created by one of our amazing volunteers.
* In most cases, you will enter the event muted.  Please default to being muted, except when you are invited to talk by the teacher/lecturer or monitor/moderator.  Zoom fails miserably when multiple people are talking at once.  It's not good for group talk and small talk.  (During the Virtual Happy Hour, you will be sent into "breakout" rooms where the number of people is small enough that you can talk more comfortably.)
* Questions are best asked via the chat. If an instructor/monitor needs you to verbally clarify a question, you will be unmuted.
* Users can choose two views in zoom: [gallery view](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/360000005883-Displaying-participants-in-gallery-view) gives you the "Brady Bunch" experience, while "speaker view" focuses on the presenter or person talking.  Except for group activities like the Virtual Happy Hour or other breakout room experiences, it usually will make sense for you to choose "speaker view."  This is very easy to do with a [toggle at the top right corner of the screen](https://support.zoom.us/hc/en-us/articles/200941109-Attendee-controls-in-a-meeting) on your computer.  It requires a lot of swiping on handheld devices.

**Zoom Help**
If you are unable to solve a zoom problem on your own, the following two volunteers are making themselves available, along with the festival director, to help you by email.  For fastest results, please send a group email to the three contacts below and put **VTF Zoom Help** in the subject line:

* Brenda: essencematpilates at gmail.com
* Jessie: jessie.c.sherwood at gmail.com
* Meredith (festival director): meredithklein at gmail.com
(Note that you have to "reassemble" each of those email addresses.)

If you have a customer service inquiry during the festival, please send it to all three emails above as well.  

If you need to submit a new registration, you will get your zoom link instantaneously if you register through our [registration site](https://secure.yogareg.com/philadelphiatango/pub?action=shopOfferingsByDate&offerTypeFilter=Drop%20In%20Class,Workshop).

If that does not work, you may pay by paypal, and send proof of payment, along with your event choices, to all three email addresses.  We commit to get back to you within five hours, but will do everything possible to respond sooner.  Thank you for your understanding.

kristin.headline: Kristin Balmer
kristin.content:
**Kristin Balmer** has been dancing tango for 22 years, and teaching for 15 years.  She started dancing in Philadelphia with Lesley Mitchell and Kelly Ray, and soon began taking yearly trips to Buenos Aires, where she studied with many teachers, including Ernesto Balmaceda, Graciela Gonzalez, and Luciana Valle.  She learned how to teach tango from Daniel Trenner, one of the first tango teachers in the U.S. (and the person who was instrumental in bringing Argentine Tango to this country).  When she moved to Baton Rouge, Kristin helped organize milongas and started teaching.  Later, Kristin moved to Nashville, where she ran Music City Tango alongside Tonya Miller, offering group & private lessons, a weekly milonga and once monthly workshops with visiting guest teachers. While in Nashville, she trained some of the dancers/teachers who now run Vanderbilt Tango.

Learning tango was not easy for Kristin.  She started as an adult, with no dance background and with an ingrained idea that she was uncoordinated.  In order to learn tango, she first had to learn how to learn. The skills she gained in the process are what make her a great teacher. She excels at helping people connect with their own bodies to improve balance and spatial awareness.  She has a knack for teaching people the subtleties of how to listen to their partners, and to give and receive information through the embrace.  She is dedicated to reframing concepts continually in order to accommodate different learning styles, and get through to every student.  She is also dedicated to building students’ confidence, as she believes people learn more readily in an environment where they feel comfortable.

A graduate of the Pennsylvania Academy of Fine Arts, Kristin’s real vocation is as an artist.  She sees tango as a hobby that has the potential to immeasurably enrich people’s lives by giving them a chance to connect with one another, to travel to other tango communities and festivals, and to lighten up and have fun.  Kristin continues to travel to tango festivals and encuentros worldwide to dance, and to connect with other tangueros.

Kristin’s classes are accessible, fun and upbeat.  Whether you have been dancing for years, or are starting from scratch, you will feel comfortable in her classes and learn a lot.

She has been on the staff of the Philadelphia Argentine Tango School since 2016.

milonga-extra-featuring-pianist-Mariano-.headline: Milonga "Extra" featuring pianist Mariano Castro
milonga-extra-featuring-pianist-Mariano-.content:
Join us for an 'extra' milonga this month to celebrate pianist **Mariano Castro** (from Buenos Aires, but living in Bucharest) during his quick fly-by through Philadelphia!  You've heard Mariano many times as the **Narcotango** pianist.  Since moving to Bucharest, his career has flourished, including a number of collaborations with symphony orchestras in major venues, such as in this video with the Filarmonica Oltenia in Romania.  (Scroll to the bottom of this page for more videos.)

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/noKLAYtW3Gg" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture" allowfullscreen></iframe>

**Saturday, January 12**

**8-9 pm:** Lecture: Tango Music & Musicality for Dancers – pianist Mariano Castro
**9 pm to midnight:** “Extra” Milonga with a concert, *“Buenos Aires from the Piano,”* by Mariano Castro at 10 pm. 

Pre-registration is now closed, but you are welcome to pay at the door:
**Workshop + Milonga with Concert:** $30
**Milonga with Concert:** $20

**BIO**

Born on July 12, 1975 in the City of Buenos Aires, Argentina, **Mariano Castro** began his musical studies in piano and guitar at age 10. In 1993 he entered in the Music Conservatory Melani (founded in 1888), studying with the director Elsa Bolia, making parallel careers in both instruments. Subsequently refined with the Russian pianist Vera Anosov, with the pianist and composer Ruben Ferrero (disciple of Antonio de Raco), with Norman Simmons (Charlie Parker, Carmen Mc Rae) in New Jersey, USA, and with the pianist and conductor Nicolás Ledesma (Leopoldo Federico, F. Suarez Paz, Julio Pane).

In his career as a performer covers various musical styles, playing in rock bands, jazz, tango, folklore and classical music, and also performing as a solo pianist and guitarist.

He has made more than 30 international tours with various groups and as soloist, performing in festivals and theaters in different cities of USA, Canada, Brazil, Ecuador, Colombia, Italy, Switzerland, Romania, Poland, Germany, Portugal, Bulgaria, Denmark, Russia and the Netherlands. Since 2008 he is part of the group “Narcotango” that fuses tango with jazz and electronic music and is recognized worldwide. With Narcotango he is nominated 2 times at the Latin Grammy awards (2009 and 2010) in the category of best tango album.

In classical music participated in two national contests of young pianists, as well as in a cycle called "Young Talents" with soprano Maria Virginia Savastano (currently at the Opera de Paris) as well as chamber groups with distinguished violinists cellists and flutists. It has also given concerts as a soloist in various parts of the world (USA, Canada, Europe) playing music by Piazzolla and others introducing the tango and the music of Argentina.

He has participated in over 20 musical productions with various artists. Upon returning from his trip to USA in 2004 he recorded his first solo album, produced by Mike Strand. In it we find the various styles in which Castro is developed closely with the piano and guitar, performing own compositions and arrangements of tangos (Villoldo, Piazzolla), folklore (G. Leguizamon) and jazz (Norman Simmons) and including some of his own compositions. Then in August 2007 released his second solo album, "Ode Porteña", a comprehensive repertoire of tangos with his own arrangements and with the participation of major artists and guest musicians. Also he is composer and producer of the latest albums made by Narcotang, Limanueva (2010, Latin Grammy nominee) and Cuenco (2013)

In their different tours (USA, Canada, Ecuador, Brazil and Europe) has also conducted workshops and clinics on the history of tango, for all audiences, where it develops exemplifying the genre's history with piano and guitar (educational concerts) . This tours in the U.S. and Canada have been sponsored and declared of cultural interest by the Ministry of Culture Office of the President and the General Directorate of Cultural Affairs of the Chancellery Argentina. In his 2006 tour in the United States he was sponsored and declared of cultural interest by the Ministry of Culture of the City of Buenos Aires. These workshops have been held in various places like universities (Drew University and Wittenberg University, USA) as well as in various cultural associations, schools and concert halls around the world.  

In 2014 Mariano and the members of narcotango were distinguished as ¨Personalidades destacadas de la cultura¨  (cultural honorary citizens) given by the Congress of Buneos Aires for their 10 years career taking this music from Buenos Aires to many other parts in the world.

Established in Europe, in Bucharest, Romania, Mariano starts the collaboration with different local musicians and orchestras. In 2015 he starts to play as soloist with different orchestras from Romania and chamber groups. The project called ¨Tango Simfonic¨ along with the argentinian singer Anlia Selis, where Mariano plays the piano and writes all the orchestral arrangements ,had a big succes on its premier at Sala Radio in Bucharest along with the Orchestra de Camera Radio Romania, and after that was played also at Brasov (with Kamerata Kronstadt), Pitesti (Orchestra Filarmonica din Pitesti) at Timisoara  (Orchestra Filarmonica Banatul)) and many others, arriving to 25 symphonic tango concerts around Romania between 2015 and 2018. He worked with the romanian conductors Radu Popa, Cristian Orosanu, Jozsef Horvath , Valentin Doni and Sabin Pautza.

In 2016 he started a new project, ArgEnTango, a tango quintet, with Analia Selis (vocals), Razvan Suma (cello), Rafael Butaru (violin) and Omar Massa (bandoneon), starting with a national tour in 10 cities of Romania playing the music of Astor Piazzolla in an authentic way, arriving to big halls and  stages like Sala Radio (Bucharest), Piata Operei (Timisoara), Filarmonica Brasov between many others. With ArgEnTango he reocrded a live album ¨Piazzolla, o poveste autentica¨ edited by Editura Casa Radio (National Radio)

In 2017 he started two new crossover symphonic projects, ¨Fits canta Piaf¨with conductor Cristian Orosanu and Alexandra Fits at vocals, playing the famous repertoire singed by Edith Piaf, and Mariano Castro, piano and orchestral arrangements. The second project ¨TanaseFits Piaf¨, also with Cristian Orosanu and Alexandra Fits, adding  some pieces of the repertoire of the great romanian singer Maria Tanase. Both projects already made several times with different philarmonic orchestras (Filarmonica Ploiesti, Orchestra de Caemra Radio, Kamerata Kronstadt, Filarmonica Pitesti, between others). By february 2018 ¨Tanase Fits Piaf¨arrived to the great Romanian Atheneum in Bucharest, this time with the Camerata Regala.

Also in 2017 and in 2018 he made two european tours with Carlos Libedinsky (Narcotango´s foundator) playing some narcotango´s music but also some new neotangos. This tour Europa Tour 2017/2018 included 20 concerts in different cities of Germany, Spain, Italy and Denmark.

how-to-help.headline: How to Help
how-to-help.content:
Tango teachers, organizers and musicians are experiencing a totally unprecedented and unexpected time of no, or severely limited, income.  Despite our active schedule of online classes, the Philadelphia Argentine Tango School is experiencing a 90% reduction in income due to Covid-19.

We have been spending much of our time applying for every funding opportunity available.  We are very fortunate to have received the commitment for a $5,000 grant from the City of Philadelphia.  We hope to have more good news to share with you soon.

Meanwhile, if you'd like to help:

* Consider joining our [online tango classes](online-class-offerings) and [our online lecture series](lecture-series-via-zoom-Wednesdays).  You can also take online tango piano lessons with Emiliano!  [Email Meredith](mailto:meredithklein@gmail.com) to set it up!

* Consider making a [tax-deductible donation](https://fundraising.fracturedatlas.org/the-philadelphia-argentine-tango-school) to the Philadelphia Argentine Tango School through our fiscal sponsor, Fractured Atlas.

* Consider giving a gift certificate.  You can [purchase on our registration site](https://secure.yogareg.com/philadelphiatango/pub?action=loginform).  We will email you a PDF gift certificate to print out or email to your giftee!

* Consider [pre-purchasing group or private lessons](https://secure.yogareg.com/philadelphiatango/pub?action=shopbundles) with Andres, Kristin or Meredith to use when the school is open again.  You can also pre-purchase piano lessons with Emiliano at the same link!  Class cards purchased now will be good for one year or longer.  Class cards already purchased will be extended by the length of this crisis.

* Consider [submitting a registration for the 2020 festival](https://www.philadelphiatangofestival.com/registration), which will be transferred to the 2021 festival, when the plan is finalized. When we re-open registration for the re-scheduled festival, prices will have to be higher. So you can save money and help us enormously by registering for the 2020 festival, even though it is not actually happening until 2021.

* If there is any way that you can ask us for an account credit instead of a refund for any activity, you will help us enormously and we will be forever grateful.

* We have virtual volunteer opportunities available, largely involving helping to promote our online activities.  Please [email Meredith](mailto:meredithklein@gmail.com) if you'd like to be involved in this way.  

* Donate to an organization working on a vaccine.  As much as I would like to think otherwise, it may be that tango cannot return to its previous level of activity unil we have one.

beginner-intensive.headline: Beginner Intensive: March 7 & 8
beginner-intensive.content:
The Beginner Intensive covers the basics of tango vocabulary, structure, technique and lead-and-follow improvisation. We change partners after every song, so there's no need to bring a partner (though you're welcome to if you have one!). The Intensive prepares students to enter directly into our Advanced Beginner Level. This intensive is open to students with no previous experience in tango. Students with experience may also take this intensive to review and reinforce the basics.

Next Intensive:

MARCH 7 & 8 - BEGINNER INTENSIVE
Session 1 - Saturday, March 7, 2-4 pm
Session 2 - Sunday, March 8, 2-4 pm
Taught by [Andres Amarilla](/andres) and [Meredith Klein](/meredith)

[REGISTER NOW](https://secure.yogareg.com/philadelphiatango/pub?action=shopOfferingsByDate&searchText=beginner%20intensive&offerTypeFilter=Drop%20In%20Class,Workshop)

Cost: 
$65/person with pre-registration
$70/person at the door
(F/T college students always get 20%.  Use code "FTstudent.")

homeTeasers.content:

homeTeasers.headline: Tango Dance & Music Immersion: Buenos Aires
homeTeasers.content:
### January 16-25, 2027

Tango's magic lies in its diversity — and nowhere is that more palpable than in Buenos Aires. Join us for 10 days immersed in the full range of today's tango: historic milongas and experimental ones, 10-piece orchestras with singers, barefoot guitar-and-bandoneón duos in the grass, Golden Age classics and bold new compositions.

Our tour includes:
🏨 Accommodation at the beautiful La Luna Lila Tango Hotel
💃 Six 1.5-hour workshops with world-renowned maestros, organized just for our group
🎶 All milongas & concerts — including live music events made possible by our tour
🚤 Group excursions, including a magical day of music, river, food and friends at Casa Azul in Tigre
📸 Professional photos to remember it all
✈️ Airport transfers, welcome & goodbye dinners

"Getting to dance and listen to so much live tango music took my dancing to another level!" — Karen, 2026 tour

$3,800 Early Bird (through 8/23) | $4,000 after
A $700 deposit holds your place and locks in your price.

Taxi dancer packages (with the best taxi dancers on earth) and single-occupancy lodging available.

Come celebrate tango's richness — and support its cutting edge.

👉 Details & sign-up: [www.milongatours.com](www.milongatours.com)
Questions? [meredithklein@gmail.com](mailto:meredithklein@gmail.com) | +1 617-291-3798

homeTeasers.headline: Safer Spaces Program
homeTeasers.content:
The Philadelphia Argentine Tango School (PATS) strives to create spaces and events that are safe, welcoming, and inclusive for everyone, regardless of age, race, gender, sexuality, ability, experience, or background.

We're very happy to launch a Safer Spaces Program to support this effort.  [Please check it out!](/safer-spaces)

Huge thanks to our Safer Spaces Coordinators, Kate Donohue and Karen Johnson, who have been working tirelessly on this effort.  They can be reached at [SaferSpacesPATS@gmail.com](mailto:SaferSpacesPATS@gmail.com).  You can report a an incident or concern [here](https://forms.gle/7y8N49sv8UQVxA7x8).
